hypothesis: The scarce-slot breakout defer edge may come from direct same-day sleeve substitution; defer a breakout only when a trend_long candidate is available on the same signal date.
change_summary: Temporarily tested a same-day trend discriminator on top of the existing default-off DEFER_BREAKOUT_WHEN_SLOTS_LTE=1 hook, then rolled the code hook back because it produced no EV improvement and gave up the known unconditional one-slot benefit.

market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull where accepted stack makes money but lags indexes
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape with lower win rate

delta_metrics.gate4_basis: Rejected because the same-day trend condition was EV-null versus baseline in all three windows and regressed two windows versus the already-known unconditional one-slot scarce-slot hook.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft ranking remains sample-limited, so this deterministic capital-allocation alpha was tested instead.

rejection_reason: Same-day trend availability does not explain the scarce-slot breakout defer edge; the useful effect appears to come from leaving capacity open for later candidates, not from same-day substitution.
next_retry_requires: Do not retry same-day trend availability as a scarce-slot breakout discriminator.
Do not combine same-day trend availability with regime allowlists or same-sector held-count crowding.
A valid scarce-slot retry needs a different information source, such as candidate-level rank gap, breadth, or forward/paper evidence.
notes: Alpha search, not measurement repair. The temporary condition hook was removed after failing Gate 4.
